title: Runtime Dispatch Semantics
text: SIMD source files use SIMDe types functions, backend names refer native target-specific staged kernel objects. configuration, optional backend accepted compiler flag SIMDe header together define expected native SIMDe macro. example, AVX2 probe compiles -mavx2, includes <simde/x86/avx2.h>, requires SIMDE_X86_AVX2_NATIVE. AVX-512 similarly requires native F, BW, VL macros. Emscripten/webR, WebAssembly SIMD128 probe compiles -msimd128, includes <simde/wasm/simd128.h>, requires SIMDE_WASM_SIMD128_NATIVE. flags make compiler define target macros __AVX2__, __AVX512BW__, __wasm_simd128__. SIMDe maps SIMDE_ARCH_* SIMDE_*_NATIVE; SIMDe function bodies use native intrinsics macros fall back native macro absent. generated src/Makevars links staged objects package shared library dispatcher, CPU feature detection, R API compiled R’s ordinary src/Makevars path. installed diagnostics report backends passed SIMDe-native compile probe: SIMDe provenance (version, commit, date) available separately via simde_info(), reads inst/vendor/simde/VERSION.

title: Runtime Dispatch Semantics
text: following benchmark evaluated article built. uses small input package checks remain fast, still exercises -process backend switching public API. switch applies full one-dimensional convolution demo: "auto" selects best backend compiled supported intersection also supports operation called. current ranking : Architecture guards mean x86 systems normally consider x86 backends, ARM systems normally consider NEON, webR/Emscripten builds can consider WebAssembly SIMD128. Scalar always available.
code: if (requireNamespace("bench", quietly = TRUE)) {   bench_x <- rep(as.raw(c(0, 1, 2, 3, 0, 255, 7, 0)), length.out = 2^20)    bench <- bench::mark(     scalar = {       simd_set_backend("scalar")       count_nonzero(bench_x)     },     auto = {       simd_set_backend("auto")       count_nonzero(bench_x)     },     iterations = 5,     check = TRUE   )    simd_set_backend("auto")   bench[, c("expression", "median", "itr/sec", "n_itr")] } #> # A tibble: 2 × 3 #>   expression   median `itr/sec` #>   <bch:expr> <bch:tm>     <dbl> #> 1 scalar        465µs     2156. #> 2 auto           48µs    19867. if (requireNamespace("bench", quietly = TRUE)) {   bench_a <- runif(10000)   bench_b <- runif(100)    conv_bench <- bench::mark(     scalar = {       simd_set_backend("scalar")       convolve1d(bench_a, bench_b)     },     auto = {       simd_set_backend("auto")       convolve1d(bench_a, bench_b)     },     iterations = 5,     check = TRUE   )    simd_set_backend("auto")   conv_bench[, c("expression", "median", "itr/sec", "n_itr")] } #> # A tibble: 2 × 3 #>   expression   median `itr/sec` #>   <bch:expr> <bch:tm>     <dbl> #> 1 scalar        411µs     2410. #> 2 auto          246µs     3991. avx512 > avx2 > sse41 > sse2 > neon > wasm_simd128 > scalar

title: Using RsimdDispatch in Other Packages
text: downstream package root: pkg omitted, package name read DESCRIPTION. prefix omitted, lowercase C identifier prefix derived package name. helper performs important substitutions, including @useDynLib, R_init_<Package>(), rsd_, RSD_, RC_ symbols. copied scaffold includes:
code: RsimdDispatch::use_simd_dispatch(pkg = "MyPackage", prefix = "mypkg") configure configure.win cleanup tools/configure-simd-dispatch.sh tools/simdDispatch/kernels/kernel_scalar.c tools/simdDispatch/kernels/kernel_sse2.c tools/simdDispatch/kernels/kernel_sse41.c tools/simdDispatch/kernels/kernel_avx2.c tools/simdDispatch/kernels/kernel_avx512.c tools/simdDispatch/kernels/kernel_neon.c tools/simdDispatch/kernels/kernel_wasm_simd128.c tools/simdDispatch/kernels/kernel_common.h src/Makevars.in src/Makevars.win.in

title: Using RsimdDispatch in Other Packages
text: demo operations registered backend file. example, AVX2 staged source registers demo operations: SSE2 SSE4.1 currently register count_nonzero(), demonstrates operation-level backend support. backend intentionally omits operation registering slot. Explicitly selecting backend allowed, calling unsupported operation errors clearly; "auto" skips backend unsupported operation. real package, replace demo operation signatures keep structure. Staged kernel files include tools/simdDispatch/kernels/kernel_api.h, private headers src/; dispatch core implements kernel-facing registration ABI. put -mavx2, -mavx512*, -msimd128, -march=native global package flags. configure helper keeps ISA flags local optional staged kernel objects, dispatcher remains safe baseline CPUs.
code: static const SdKernelDef sd_avx2_kernels[] = {     {SD_OP_COUNT_NONZERO, SD_SIG_RAW_COUNT, sd_count_nonzero_avx2_invoke},     {SD_OP_CONVOLVE1D, SD_SIG_F64_CONVOLVE, sd_convolve1d_avx2_invoke},     SD_KERNEL_DEF_END };  void sd_register_avx2(SdDispatchBuilder *builder) {     sd_register_kernel_table(builder, sd_avx2_kernels); } R API wrapper        ordinary src/Makevars compilation CPU feature checks   ordinary src/Makevars compilation dispatch table       ordinary src/Makevars compilation scalar kernel        staged by configure under src/rsd-kernels/ SSE/AVX/NEON/wasm files   staged by configure as optional objects under src/rsd-kernels/

title: Using RsimdDispatch in Other Packages
text: staged build model already scales across operations: add another function existing tools/simdDispatch/kernels/kernel_*.c file, staged object backend contains new implementation. small number operations, keep dispatch code explicit add: operation ID, signature ID, call-frame type, kernel definition entry type support tools/simdDispatch/kernels/kernel_api.h; one implementation plus small void * call-frame thunk per backend file tools/simdDispatch/kernels/, following naming convention sd_<operation>_<backend>(); one SdKernelDef row backend file implements operation; .Call wrapper src/r_api.c, marshals R objects operation call frame calls sd_dispatch_invoke(); native registration entry src/registration.c; R wrapper, tests, documentation. Backend metadata kernel availability table-driven, similar spirit R’s native routine registration tables. Operation implementation ownership stays close backend source file, tools/simdDispatch/simd_dispatch.c consumes opaque operation IDs generic kernel-definition rows. packages many public operations, keep single operation catalog can generate repetitive dispatch metadata, .Call registration, R wrappers. Optimized backend kernels can remain hand-written, operation names, argument lists, exported wrapper metadata duplicated hand.

what: Runtime SIMD Dispatch Templates for C Code in R Packages
title: Runtime SIMD Dispatch Templates for C Code in R Packages
text: Pure-C runtime SIMD dispatch templates R packages. Stage scalar, SSE, AVX, AVX-512, NEON, WebAssembly SIMD128 kernel objects configuration; link one shared library switch among compiled CPU-supported implementations runtime.

title: Runtime SIMD Dispatch Templates for C Code in R Packages

title: Runtime SIMD Dispatch Templates for C Code in R Packages
text: Copy C dispatch scaffold package root: helper updates DESCRIPTION LinkingTo: RsimdDispatch, writes scaffold files, substitutes package-specific registration C symbol prefixes. add runtime dependency RsimdDispatch. Replace demo count_nonzero() convolve1d() kernels tools/kernels/ scalar/SSE/AVX/NEON/WebAssembly kernels. generated configure script probes compiler support, stages selected kernel objects src/rsd-kernels/, leaves src/Makevars link objects baseline R API, CPU detection, dispatcher. See package vignettes downstream-package workflow dispatch semantics.

title: Runtime SIMD Dispatch Templates for C Code in R Packages
text: simd_set_backend() rejects uncompiled CPU-unsupported backends. Operation-level backend availability reported simd_info()$operation_backends, active resolved backend operation reported simd_info()$operation_selected_backends.
code: library(RsimdDispatch)  x <- as.raw(c(0, 1, 2, 0, 255)) a <- c(1, 2, 3) b <- c(10, 100)  count_nonzero(x) #> [1] 3 convolve1d(a, b) #> [1]  10 120 230 300 simd_backend() #> [1] "avx2" simd_set_backend("scalar") simd_backend() #> [1] "scalar" simd_set_backend("avx2") simd_backend() #> [1] "avx2" simd_set_backend("auto") simd_info()[c("compiled_backends", "available_backends", "operation_selected_backends", "simde_native_backends")] #> $compiled_backends #> [1] "scalar" "sse2"   "sse41"  "avx2"   "avx512" #> #> $available_backends #> [1] "scalar" "sse2"   "sse41"  "avx2" #> #> $operation_selected_backends #> count_nonzero    convolve1d #>        "avx2"        "avx2" #> #> $simde_native_backends #> [1] "sse2"   "sse41"  "avx2"   "avx512" simde_info()[c("version", "commit")] #> $version #> [1] "0.8.4" #> #> $commit #> [1] "f3e8262173b7089db9a9d57a9ecef8dd07ad9c97" simd_dispatch_template_path() #> [1] "/usr/local/lib/R/site-library/RsimdDispatch/templates/dispatch-c"

title: Runtime SIMD Dispatch Templates for C Code in R Packages
text: Switching backends -process variants linked one R shared library selected guarded resolved operation table.
code: count_nonzero(x) #> [1] 3 convolve1d(a, b) #> [1]  10 120 230 300 simd_info()[c("selected_backend", "operation_selected_backends", "available_backends")] #> $selected_backend #> [1] "avx2" #> #> $operation_selected_backends #> count_nonzero    convolve1d #>        "avx2"        "avx2" #> #> $available_backends #> [1] "scalar" "sse2"   "sse41"  "avx2" simd_set_backend("scalar") scalar_count <- count_nonzero(x) scalar_conv <- convolve1d(a, b)  simd_set_backend("avx2") avx2_count <- count_nonzero(x) avx2_conv <- convolve1d(a, b)  simd_set_backend("auto") data.frame(   scalar = scalar_count,   avx2 = avx2_count,   convolution_equal = identical(scalar_conv, avx2_conv),   selected_after_auto = simd_backend() ) #>   scalar avx2 convolution_equal selected_after_auto #> 1      3    3              TRUE                avx2

title: Runtime SIMD Dispatch Templates for C Code in R Packages
text: runtime switch can benchmark full one-dimensional convolution: classic nested-loop [+ j - 1] += [] * b[j] kernel.
code: set.seed(1) x <- as.raw(sample.int(256L, 50 * 1024 * 1024, replace = TRUE) - 1L)  mark <- bench::mark(   scalar = {     simd_set_backend("scalar")     count_nonzero(x)   },   avx2 = {     simd_set_backend("avx2")     count_nonzero(x)   },   iterations = 20,   check = TRUE,   memory = FALSE )  simd_set_backend("auto")  bench <- data.frame(   backend = as.character(mark$expression),   median_ms = as.numeric(mark$median) * 1000,   mb_per_second = length(x) * as.numeric(mark$`itr/sec`) / 1e6,   iterations = mark$n_itr,   row.names = NULL ) bench$speedup_vs_scalar <- bench$mb_per_second / bench$mb_per_second[bench$backend == "scalar"]  knitr::kable(bench, digits = 3) set.seed(2) a <- runif(100000) b <- runif(100)  conv_mark <- bench::mark(   scalar = {     simd_set_backend("scalar")     convolve1d(a, b)   },   avx2 = {     simd_set_backend("avx2")     convolve1d(a, b)   },   iterations = 20,   check = TRUE,   memory = FALSE )  simd_set_backend("auto")  conv_bench <- data.frame(   backend = as.character(conv_mark$expression),   median_ms = as.numeric(conv_mark$median) * 1000,   million_multiply_adds_per_second = (length(a) * length(b)) * as.numeric(conv_mark$`itr/sec`) / 1e6,   iterations = conv_mark$n_itr,   row.names = NULL ) conv_bench$speedup_vs_scalar <- conv_bench$million_multiply_adds_per_second /   conv_bench$million_multiply_adds_per_second[conv_bench$backend == "scalar"]  knitr::kable(conv_bench, digits = 3)

what: Full one-dimensional convolution with the selected SIMD backend — convolve1d
title: Full one-dimensional convolution with the selected SIMD backend — convolve1d
text: Demonstration numeric kernel runtime dispatch template. convolve1d() computes full convolution simple nested-loop R definition. pair positions adds [] * b[j] [+ j - 1]. SIMD backends vectorize inner multiply-add b shifted output window.

what: Report runtime SIMD dispatch diagnostics — simd_info
title: Report runtime SIMD dispatch diagnostics — simd_info
text: Returns requested backend, selected backend, compiled backends, CPU-supported backends, operation-level backend availability, operation-level selected backends, SIMDe-native backends, target architecture information. Calling initializes lazy auto-dispatch selection already initialized. SIMDe provenance (version, commit, date) use simde_info().

title: Report runtime SIMD dispatch diagnostics — simd_info
Report runtime SIMD dispatch diagnostics — simd_info
text: named list following elements: dispatch_mode Character scalar. Internal dispatch implementation strategy string (stable across patch releases). requested_backend Character scalar. last value passed simd_set_backend(), "auto" explicitly set. selected_backend Character scalar. Summary currently active backend: backend name, "mixed", "unavailable". compiled_backends Character vector. Backends compiled shared library (necessarily supported current CPU). cpu_supported_backends Character vector. Backends whose required CPU features present runtime. available_backends Character vector. Backends compiled CPU-supported (.e. usable). simde_native_backends Character vector. Backends compiler emitted native intrinsics rather SIMDe emulation. operations Character vector. Names registered operations (e.g. "count_nonzero", "convolve1d"). operation_backends Named list character vectors. operation, backends provide kernel . operation_selected_backends Named character vector. operation, backend currently selected dispatch (NA none resolved). cpu_sse2 Logical scalar. TRUE SSE2 detected runtime. cpu_sse41 Logical scalar. TRUE SSE4.1 detected. cpu_avx2 Logical scalar. TRUE AVX2 detected. cpu_avx512 Logical scalar. TRUE AVX-512 (F/BW/VL) detected. cpu_neon Logical scalar. TRUE NEON/AdvSIMD detected. cpu_wasm_simd128 Logical scalar. TRUE WASM SIMD128 available. target_arch Character scalar. Compiler target CPU architecture (e.g. "x86_64", "aarch64"). target_os Character scalar. Compiler target OS family (e.g. "linux", "macos", "windows", "emscripten").

title: Report runtime SIMD dispatch diagnostics — simd_info

what: Select the runtime SIMD backend — simd_set_backend
title: Select the runtime SIMD backend — simd_set_backend
text: Select backend used subsequent calls dispatched demo kernels count_nonzero() convolve1d(). RsimdDispatch keeps compiled variants one shared object switches guarded resolved operation table. makes -process benchmarking possible.

title: Select the runtime SIMD backend — simd_set_backend
Select the runtime SIMD backend — simd_set_backend
text: backend Character scalar. Use "auto" select best available backend, one simd_info()$available_backends explicit choice.

title: Select the runtime SIMD backend — simd_set_backend
text: selected backend summary string, invisibly. Possible values: "<name>" operations resolved named backend. "mixed" Different operations resolved different backends (possible "auto"). "unavailable" operation resolved requested backend.

title: Select the runtime SIMD backend — simd_set_backend

title: Report vendored SIMDe provenance — simde_info
RsimdDispatch 0.1.2.9000
text: Open post-0.1.2 development cycle. Clarify many-operation maintenance guidance: repetitive dispatch metadata, C registration, R wrapper metadata come one source truth; hand-written code focus backend kernels. Move downstream-use vignette runtime dispatch semantics pkgdown article order shorten many-operation guidance. Trim redundant backend-selection wording README dispatch semantics documentation. Move staged-kernel-kernel_common.h helper src/ tools/kernels/ copied template kernel directory, update downstream-template docs layout. Replace short-lived C preprocessor expansion blocks src/ explicit operation-table declarations, dispatch wrappers, native registration entries. Move dispatch NumKong-inspired resolved operation table: backend files register operation slots implement, "auto" resolves best backend per operation, simd_info() reports operation-level backend availability selected backend operation. Add Dockerfile.musl-check Alpine Linux / musl R CMD check smoke testing repository-specific CI. Split staged-kernel registration ABI tools/kernels/kernel_api.h backend kernels longer include private dispatch headers src/. Backend files now expose R-style RsdKernelDef tables consumed generic dispatch core, keeping operation names operation-specific wrappers src/simd_dispatch.c.

title: RsimdDispatch 0.1.2
text: Add WebAssembly SIMD128 (wasm_simd128) staged-kernel support Emscripten/webR builds using SIMDe’s <simde/wasm/simd128.h> backend SIMDE_WASM_SIMD128_NATIVE configure probing. Extend simd_info() diagnostics cpu_wasm_simd128 include wasm_simd128 compiled, available, SIMDe-native backend sets built SIMD128-capable WebAssembly toolchain. Add webR check tooling based local rwasm::build() + Node webr workflow used related packages. Generate explicit SIMDe include flags src/Makevars quote configure include paths copied templates robust downstream libraries. Eagerly initialize dispatch package load, exercise copied-template runtime behavior CI, remove legacy comma-separated backend-list C helpers. Switch dispatch one function pointer per operation backend RsdOps operation table. Backend diagnostics now reuse C backend metadata table, R setter longer hardcodes backend names. Add dispatched convolve1d() full one-dimensional convolution demo, using SIMDe inner-loop multiply-add kernels numeric vectors, include tests, documentation, webR checks, evaluated benchmarks. Document current extension path adding operations retaining RsimdDispatch’s staged-object R package build model.

title: RsimdDispatch 0.1.1 (2026-05-28)
text: Stage scalar optional SIMD kernel objects configure, link generated src/Makevars baseline R API, CPU feature detection, dispatcher code. Move demo kernels src/ tools/kernels/ copied templates keep build-time kernel sources separate ordinary R package src/ files. Reword DESCRIPTION avoid incoming spell-check notes backend acronym names keeping detailed backend names user-facing documentation.

title: RsimdDispatch 0.1.0
text: Initial release. Provides working runtime SIMD dispatch example R packages, using one shared library scalar, SSE2, SSE4.1, AVX2, AVX-512, NEON kernel objects supported compiler CPU. Exports count_nonzero(), simd_set_backend(), simd_backend(), simd_info() demonstrate safe -process backend selection diagnostics. Adds use_simd_dispatch() simd_dispatch_template_path() package authors want copy adapt C dispatch scaffold. Vendors header-SIMDe library downstream packages LinkingTo: RsimdDispatch. Adds simde_info() SIMDe fields simd_info() report vendored SIMDe version pinned upstream commit.
